User Authentication Flow

Annotated Flowchart · plan-html · Click any step to inspect
1
User submits credentials
POST /auth/v1/token · email + password
2
Supabase Auth verifies identity
bcrypt hash comparison · rate-limit check
Branch: ❌ Invalid credentials → 400 🔐 MFA required → Step 2b
3
Issue access + refresh tokens
JWT (1h TTL) · Refresh token (30d) · HttpOnly cookie
4
Middleware validates session
getUser() on every RSC render · no getSession()
Branch: ⏰ Token expired → auto-refresh 🚫 Invalid → redirect /login
5
RLS enforces data access
auth.uid() bound to row-level policies · no server-side filter needed
Step 1 — User submits credentials
Credentials are sent over HTTPS as JSON body. Never log or store raw passwords. Rate limiting is enforced at the Supabase edge before this request reaches the auth handler.
Implementation: supabase.auth.signInWithPassword({ email, password })
